tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, with elevated creatinine suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, elevated bilirubin, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance/color, indicating possible renal and/or hepatic dysfunction. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further evaluation for renal and hepatic pathology and review of glycemic management.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
